items.title: Crowds gather to bid Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik farewell in quiet ceremony
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — A massive crowd turned up to bid a final farewell to former MCA president Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at Xiao En Centre in Cheras today. Flower wreaths line the floor during the wake of former transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at Xiao En Centre, Cheras, April 8, 2026. Friends, family members and political figures gather to pay their final respects during the wake of former transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at Xiao En Centre, Cheras, April 8, 2026.

items.title: U Mobile reaches 80pct 5G coverage in under nine months
items.summary: In light of this, the company received recognition from the Malaysia Book of Records for the fastest 5G network rollout in the country. U Mobile said the same discipline and execution applied in its outdoor rollout are also guiding its in-building coverage (IBC) deployment, with the company on track to reach 175 IBC sites by July 2026. "U Mobile has a long track record of industry recognition, including being an eight-time winner of Frost & Sullivan's Malaysian Mobile Services Company of the Year award, as well as Best Prepaid and Best Postpaid in 2025.

items.title: Boy with congenital heart condition seeks RM30k for surgery at IJN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A five-year-old boy suffering from a congenital heart condition is seeking RM30,000 to undergo surgery at Institut Jantung Negara (IJN). Muhammad Haffaz Nufail Mohd Hariz Hilmi has been diagnosed with a ventricular septal defect (VSD), a condition involving a hole in the heart that requires surgical closure. His mother, Nurul Azimah Abdul Aziz, 32, said the condition was first detected shortly after his birth during a hospital check-up.

items.title: Security guard charged with raping 14-year-old girlfriend
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A security guard was charged in the Sessions Court here today with raping his 14-year-old girlfriend two months ago. Muhammad Syafiq Thajudin, 25, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before Judge Datin Surita Budin. Deputy public prosecutor Nurul Balkis Zunaidi proposed bail at RM15,000 with one surety, along with additional conditions.

items.title: Palm drops more than 3pct as crude oil slumps on US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil futures tumbled more than three per cent on Wednesday, pressured by a sharp drop in crude oil prices following a US-Iran ceasefire deal. Palm oil prices are currently tracking developments in the Middle East conflict closely, mirroring movements in crude oil prices, said Sandeep Singh, director of The Farm Trade, a Kuala Lumpur-based consulting and trading company. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract fell 2.73 per cent, while its palm oil contract shed 3.76 per cent.

items.title: Vietnam set for US$6 billion inflows after securing long-awaited emerging market status
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam's stock market regulator expects FTSE Russell's long-awaitedconfirmation of the country's emerging market status will help draw foreign capital back to a market that has suffered from steady selling this year. FTSE Russell announced on Tuesday it will upgrade Vietnam to emerging market status from frontier status in September, when it will start adding the country to its global equity indexes in phases. FTSE Russell said adding US$6 billion in foreign capital would give Vietnam a weighting of as much as 0.35 per cent in its emerging market index, with conglomerate Vingroup, Masan Group , FPT Corp and Hoa Phat among potential inclusions.

items.title: Israel supports US-Iran ceasefire, excludes Lebanon from deal
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel said Wednesday it supported US President Donald Trump's decision to suspend his bombing of Iran, but maintained the 11th-hour ceasefire did not include Lebanon. Washington and Teheran agreed to a two-week ceasefire Tuesday, barely an hour before Trump's deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire. "Israel supports President Trump's decision to suspend strikes against Iran for two weeks subject to Iran immediately opening the straits and stopping all attacks on the US, Israel and countries in the region," the office of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said in a statement.

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader says China visit to sow 'seeds of peace'
items.summary: NANJING: Taiwan's opposition leader, a proponent of closer ties with Beijing, said on Wednesday she hoped to sow the "seeds of peace" during a rare visit to China. Kuomintang (KMT) chairwoman Cheng Li-wun is the party's first leader to visit China in a decade but her trip – during which she hopes to meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping – has sparked debate in Taiwan. China claims self-ruled Taiwan is part of its territory and has threatened to use force to seize it.

items.title: SK Hynix shares jump 15pct after peer Samsung projects blowout earnings
items.summary: SEOUL: Shares of South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix surged on Wednesday after peer Samsung Electronics forecast market-beating quarterly earnings, fuelling expectations about SK Hynix's results. SK Hynix shares traded up 15 per cent at 1,050,000 won (US$712.20) each, outperforming Samsung's 8.7 per cent gain and the wider market's seven per cent rise. SK Hynix, the world's second-biggest memory chip maker after Samsung Electronics, is set to report January-March earnings later this month.

items.title: "A big day for world peace," says Trump on US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: US President Donald Trump said it is "a big day for world peace" following a two-week ceasefire agreement reached between the United States and Iran. In a social media post, Trump claimed the agreement was reached because Iran "wants it to happen" and has "had enough". In the same post, Trump said Iran could begin the reconstruction process and that the US would assist in managing traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: TikTok to build a second billion-euro data centre in Finland
items.summary: HELSINKI/STOCKHOLM: TikTok plans to invest one billion euros (US$1.16 billion) to build a second data centre in Finland in less than a year as it moves data storage for European users to the continent, company officials said on Wednesday. TikTok said it was making a new one billion euro investment in a data centre with an initial capacity of 50 megawatts (MW) and a total potential capacity of 128MW in Lahti, located in southern Finland. The investment is part of the company's "12 billion (euro) European data sovereignty initiative delivering industry-leading protections for the data of over 200 million European users," it told Reuters.

items.title: Asian stocks, currencies rally as US-Iran ceasefire lifts risk appetite
items.summary: The MSCI gauge of EM Asia equities surged five per cent to a three-week high. A broader index of global EM equities jumped four per cent to a three-week high, while its currency counterpart advanced nearly one per cent. Stocks in Singapore, Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, and Thailand also jumped between one per cent and three per cent.

items.title: India's Titan hits record high on strong fourth-quarter sales
items.summary: BENGALURU: Shares of Indian jeweller Titan Company rose as much as 5.7 per cent to hit a record high on Wednesday, after posting a 42 per cent rise in quarterly domestic sales, driven by higher average selling prices for gold and a rise in buyer volumes. Titan's mainstay jewellery business, which accounts for over 90 per cent of its revenue, grew 46 per cent year-on-year, the company said in a business update for the quarter ended March 31. Spot gold prices rose nearly eight per cent during the quarter.

items.title: MCMC investigates 36 fake news cases on energy crisis: Strict action promised
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has opened 36 investigation papers into the spread of fake news on social and digital media related to the global energy crisis. “Six investigation papers have been referred to the deputy public prosecutor for review and further action,” it said. MCMC said it takes seriously any misuse of social and digital media platforms aimed at misleading the public through false information.

items.title: RM5m graft probe involving research funds: MACC remands four, including lecturers
items.summary: IPOH, April 8 — Four individuals, including two lecturers from a private higher education institution in northern Malaysia, have been remanded for four days to assist in an investigation into the solicitation and receipt of approximately RM5 million in bribes. Four individuals, including two lecturers from a northern private higher education institution, have been remanded for four days over an alleged RM5 million bribery case in Ipoh April 8, 2026. — Bernama pic.

items.title: Third canopy bridge in Penang aims to reduce roadkill, ease human-monkey conflict
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A third artificial road canopy bridge has been installed in Penang to reduce roadkill and address rising human-monkey conflict in urban areas. Known as The Obscura, the new structure was unveiled today by Langur Project Penang (LPP) under its Bridge to Coexist initiative, which allows arboreal wildlife to move safely between fragmented habitats. LPP head and primatologist Dr Yap Jo Leen said the project reflects a science-based, community-driven approach to conservation.

items.title: #NSTviral: Visitor safety top priority amid macaque presence at Zoo Taiping
items.summary: TAIPING: Visitor safety remains the top priority amid the continued presence of wild macaques roaming within Zoo Taiping and Night Safari's open-concept environment. Taiping Municipal Council president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said the macaques frequently seen in the zoo are not part of its animal collection, but wild animals that have long entered the area in search of food. "They are attracted by food sources, including food provided to animals and items carried by visitors," he said when met at his office today.

items.title: Four strangers become heroes, save doctor from drowning in monsoon drain
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The swift actions of four men saved a doctor from drowning after her car plunged into a large monsoon drain here yesterday. The victim was driving her car along Jalan Lintas Kepayan from the Kota Kinabalu International Airport towards Lido when the incident occurred about 7pm. The tow truck driver, who witnessed the accident, immediately leapt into the drain to assist.

items.title: ‘Just to share blessings’: Sponsor defends crying competition in Kelantan after backlash
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 8 — A sponsor of a crying competition in Kelantan has defended the event after it drew criticism online, saying participants joined voluntarily and the aim was simply to “share blessings”. Entrepreneur Wan Mohamad Rahimie Syazrin Ab Rahim said his company was the main sponsor of the three-day expo at RTC Tunjung, where the competition was held as one of several activities, according to a report in Berita Harian . The 32-year-old reportedly said the competition attracted 60 participants aged between 30 and 70, and also included a children’s category.

items.title: Fama rolls out intervention plan to address global energy crisis impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Agriculture and Food Security Ministry, through the Federal Agricultural Marketing Authority (Fama), today announced a comprehensive Supply and Marketing Intervention Plan for agricultural produce to address the impact of the global energy crisis. Under the plan, Fama will strengthen the Agro Madani Sale programme by expanding the 'Jimat Dapur Rakyat' initiative, targeting 2,399 sessions nationwide from April to September this year. The programme will also be conducted continuously at Fama-run premises, including Fama Agrobazaar, Fama Operation Centres and other strategic locations.

items.title: Malaysia welcomes US-Iran ceasefire agreement
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia welcomes the two-week ceasefire agreement reached between the United States (US) and Iran, the Foreign Ministry said in a statement Wednesday. It said the significant development serves as a crucial step towards de-escalating tensions and restoring much-needed peace and stability to the West Asia region. "Malaysia expresses its appreciation to all parties involved in the diplomatic efforts and mediations that led to this agreement.

items.title: #NSTviral: Viral video of pedestrian struck by motorcycle raises road safety alarm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An incident involving a pedestrian along Jalan Yap Kwan Seng has sparked concern after a video of the episode went viral on social media. The clip , widely circulated on Threads, showed a woman being struck by a motorcycle while attempting to cross the road, with many alleging that the rider had been travelling against the flow of traffic. Several bystanders rushed to assist the victim as traffic slowed around the scene.

items.title: US lawmakers call for Trump's removal despite two-week Iran ceasefire
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US lawmaker John Larson announced Tuesday that he filed articles of impeachment against President Donald Trump, citing escalating concerns over his conduct and handling of the war with Iran. "Donald Trump has blown past every requirement to be removed from office. "Donald Trump can't simply threaten war crimes with impunity.

items.title: Trump: Iran deal 'total and complete victory' for US
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump told AFP on Tuesday that the United States had won a "total and complete victory" after agreeing a two-week ceasefire deal with Iran. In a telephone call, Trump said he believed China had persuaded Iran to negotiate, and said Teheran's enriched uranium would be "perfectly taken care of." We'll see if it gets there," Trump said.

items.title: Dollar drops as Iran ceasefire prompts risk-on turn for markets
items.summary: The yen strengthened 0.8 per cent against the greenback to 158.36 per dollar. The euro was up 0.7 per cent at US$1.1674, while the British pound appreciated 0.8 per cent to US$1.34. The Australian dollar climbed 1.1 per cent to US$0.7054.

items.title: ‘Not favouritism’: Sabah leaders defend federal diesel subsidy, say RM2.15 rate is a necessity
items.summary: Sabah Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said difficult terrain and limited road infrastructure make diesel-powered vehicles, particularly four-wheel drives, a necessity compared to Peninsular Malaysia. Meanwhile, former Sabah Youth and Sports Minister Tan Sri Liew Yun Fah said the subsidy is not unfair to other states. The Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah (PHRS) president said the move is justified given differences in basic facilities, road conditions and higher logistics costs.

items.title: Both sides claim victory after US, Iran agree to 11th-hour truce
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire barely an hour before President Donald Trump's Wednesday deadline to obliterate the country was set to expire, with Teheran to temporarily reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz. Israel had encouraged Trump to join the war against Iran, its arch-nemesis, and in the first strikes killed the long-serving supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Trump had set a deadline to Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz by 8pm Washington time (0000 GMT Wednesday), or 3.30am in Teheran.

items.title: HLIB keeps 'Buy' on Sunway, cuts target price after IJM deal lapse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) has maintained its "Buy" call on Sunway Bhd, but lowered its target price to RM5.54 following the lapse of its conditional takeover offer for IJM Corp Bhd. It said Sunway's healthcare arm, Sunway Healthcare Holdings Bhd, has performed strongly since its listing on March 18, with its share price rising to RM1.90, up 31 per cent from its initial public offering price of RM1.45. Despite this, Sunway's own share price remains relatively modest, suggesting that the embedded value of its healthcare business has yet to be fully reflected.

items.title: Thai man arrested for petrol smuggling at Bukit Kayu Hitam border
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM: The Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) detained a Thai national on Monday suspected of involvement in smuggling petrol into a neighbouring country. Bukit Kayu Hitam MCBA commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Fauzi Mohd Yusoff said the suspect was arrested at Block B in the outbound lane of the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine, and Security (ICQS) Complex here about 10.45pm. "Upon inspecting a man acting suspiciously inside a Thai-registered vehicle, authorities discovered modifications to the rear and seating areas, believed to be used for petrol smuggling.

items.title: RHB Research raises Aeon Credit target price after strong FY26 results
items.summary: It said Aeon Credit's key performance indicators for FY27 appear modest, likely reflecting uncertainties stemming from ongoing geopolitical tensions. For FY27, Aeon Credit is targeting loan growth of eight per cent, return on equity (ROE) of about 12 per cent, lower than 13.1 per cent in FY26, and a dividend payout exceeding 30 per cent. We project ROE of 13.6 per cent and assume a payout ratio of 40 per cent," RHB Research said.

items.title: KPDN summons company over viral LPG price hike claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has taken note of a viral message regarding adjustments to liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) prices by a company in Negeri Sembilan. In a Facebook post, KPDN said the price adjustment involved commercial gas cylinders and not subsidised domestic gas cylinders. “Nevertheless, immediate action has been taken to summon the company concerned to obtain an explanation in accordance with the Control of Supplies Act 1961 (Act 122) and the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 (Act 723),” it said.

items.title: Eldest son misses Dr Ling funeral due to treatment abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The eldest son of the late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, Ling Hee Leong, was unable to attend his father's funeral due to ongoing medical treatment in Australia. His younger brother, Ling Hee Keat, said Hee Leong is currently undergoing a six-week course of treatment and has been advised by doctors not to interrupt it. Hee Keat added that his brother had returned to Kuala Lumpur several times recently to visit their father, with his last visit taking place about two weeks ago.

items.title: Malaysia welcomes Iran-US ten-point peace plan, urges lasting resolution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has welcomed the latest development in the ongoing US-Iran conflict. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim described the ten-point plan proposed by Iran, which has been positively received by the United States, as a "significant step towards peace and stability". "This proposal augurs well for the restoration of peace and stability, not only in the region but also globally.

items.title: Empire Premium poised for steady earnings growth post listing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Empire Premium Food Bhd is projected to post core net earnings of RM44.2 million in financial year 2026 (FY26) and RM49.1 million in FY27. "The group has allocated RM79.1 million to fund the opening of 56 new outlets between FY27 and FY29. Citing industry research by Vital Factor Consulting, Rakuten said Empire Premium's net margin of 16 per cent outpaces its closest listed peers, including Sushi King at 1.9 per cent and Sushi Zanmai at 11.2 per cent.

items.title: Court rules bank partly liable for failing to stop scam on elderly customer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court in Johor Baru has found CIMB Bank Bhd partly liable for failing to stop a scam that drained more than RM500,000 from an elderly customer's account over two weeks. The court held that while the Ng who is now deceased had been deceived into revealing his debit card Personal Identification Number (PIN) and surrendering his card to scammers, the bank bore a greater responsibility for allowing the fraud to continue unchecked. However, the judge said the bank could not avoid responsibility entirely.

items.title: CIMB offers payment relief to customers hit by Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Bank Bhd and CIMB Islamic Bank Bhd will provide targeted support measures for individual and business customers affected by ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Business customers can also access tailored restructuring and rescheduling options to help manage repayment obligations and sustain operations. Group chief executive officer Novan Amirudin said CIMB remains committed to supporting customers as they navigate evolving challenges, ensuring financial assistance remains accessible.

items.title: Form Three boy dies in three-motorcycle Terengganu crash, police link incident to ‘wheelie’ stunt
items.summary: BANDAR PERMAISURI, April 8 — A 15-year-old Form Three student reportedly died after being struck by another motorcycle while on the road following a crash in Kampung Pelong, Sungai Tong, Terengganu. According to Bernama , Setiu district police chief Superintendent Mohd Zain Mat Dris said initial investigations found the teenager had been riding a Honda C100 motorcycle and is believed to have been performing a dangerous stunt by lifting the front wheel — also known as a “wheelie”. Another motorcycle travelling in the same direction was unable to avoid him and struck him.

items.title: Anwar hails Pakistan-brokered truce between Iran and US as key to restoring global peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today welcomed the just-announced ceasefire between the United States and Iran, hailing it as a crucial step towards restoring global peace and urging that the talks lead to a lasting resolution for the entire region, including an end to the “genocide” in Palestine. Anwar specifically referenced a ten-point peace plan proposed by Iran, which he noted was “positively received by the US.” “This proposal augurs well for the restoration of peace and stability, not only to the region but also the rest of the world,” Anwar said. “Let this be a turning point towards lasting peace.

items.title: No extra BUDI95 applications: MOF denies baseless claims, says 200-litre monthly cap remains
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — Claims of additional BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) subsidy application made available for the general public are untrue and baseless, says the Ministry of Finance (MOF). In a statement yesterday, MOF said that although the function existed during the early implementation of BUDI95, it was not intended for additional applications based on personal needs. “Following this, the application button has been deactivated,” the statement said.

items.title: Police hunt Proton Saga driver over six-vehicle crash in Melaka Tengah
items.summary: MELAKA, April 8 — Police are tracking down the driver of a Proton Saga believed to have caused a six-vehicle crash along Lebuh AMJ Cheng Heights, Melaka Tengah, yesterday morning. “The Proton Saga then spun and hit several other vehicles, including a Toyota Vios, Perodua Bezza, a Nissan van and a Toyota Camry, bringing the total to six vehicles involved,” he said in a statement yesterday, adding that the driver was not at the scene and has yet to be identified. Police are tracing the driver by checking vehicle ownership, identifying witnesses and obtaining CCTV and dashcam footage.

items.title: Penang Islamic dept halts temple’s Hari Raya open house, says celebration is religious
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 8 — The Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIPP) has ordered a Chinese temple association to cancel its planned Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house, saying the event is a Muslim religious activity that falls under the purview of the State Islamic Religious Council. “As] the celebration of Hari Raya Aidilfitri is one of the religious activities of the Muslim community, the Thean Hock Keong Association is therefore not permitted to organise an Open House Ceremony in conjunction with the said Hari Raya Aidilfitri,” the letter said. “For the information of the Thean Hock Keong Association, all Islamic religious activities including any celebration related to Hari Raya Aidilfitri are subject to the approval of the Penang State Islamic Religious Council.” Association clarifies unity-focused intent In a detailed response dated April 5, Thean Hock Keong’s secretary, Ng Choong Seong, expressed regret over the cancellation while clarifying the event’s purpose.

items.title: Police detain 12 men over Cheras entertainment centre riot where lorry was used to ram gate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Police have detained 12 men to assist in investigations into a riot and mischief at an entertainment centre in Cheras here last Sunday. Kuala Lumpur Police Chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said several vehicles believed to have been used in the incident were also seized, including a lorry that was used to ram the premises’ main gate. He said all suspects had been arrested and remand applications had been made to facilitate further investigations.

items.title: Police: Preliminary probe finds gang dispute behind Ipoh murder, not 3R issues
items.summary: IPOH, April 8 — A gang dispute is believed to have led to the murder of a man in Buntong here last night, police said. Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said preliminary investigations found the incident involved conflict between several members of a certain group and was unrelated to racial issues. The man was killed during a fight at about 11.10pm Monday, according to Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah.

items.title: Fahmi ‘shocked’ by Papagomo posts after lawsuit settlement, seeks legal advice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The legal team for Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil is examining recent social media comments by Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as “Papagomo”, following the settlement of a lawsuit between them yesterday. Fahmi said he had received feedback from various parties regarding Wan Muhammad Azri’s latest statements and would seek legal advice to determine whether they breached the terms of the court settlement. “Yesterday, two of the three cases in this matter were settled, the first with Mohd Fauzan Madzlan, the second with Wan Muhammad Azri, and the third with Ahmad Dusuki Abd Rani.

items.title: MMEA ramps up diesel smuggling crackdown to curb subsidy leakages in national waters
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening and intensifying enforcement operations in national waters to crack down on smuggling and illegal oil transfers, including unauthorised bunkering (supplying fuel to ships) activities. MMEA director-general, Maritime Admiral Mohd Rosli Abdullah, said enforcement operations are conducted firmly and systematically, in close coordination with other agencies, to plug any gaps that could threaten national interests. “MMEA is ready to lead maritime efforts to curb leakage and smuggling of subsidised diesel, following the government’s announcement on Monday,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: MACC arrests four in Perak, including two lecturers, over RM5m bribes linked to university research funds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) arrested four individuals, including two private university lecturers, for allegedly soliciting and receiving about RM5 million in bribes. An MACC source said that the two lecturers, an oil company officer and an engineering company director were arrested at the Perak MACC office between 2pm to 5pm yesterday. The officer and the company director are believed to have acted as proxies for both the lecturers to receive illicit funds.

items.title: Perhilitan seizes RM36.8m Johor haul: Compressed tiger parts, primate parts and suspected python genitalia among contraband
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) seized a large haul of various exotic wildlife parts worth RM36.8 million at a house in Taman Johor Jaya here last Saturday. The raid netted thousands of exotic wildlife parts that are believed to be destined for the international illicit wildlife trade. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the department’s enforcement team found 602 python bile, 420 bear bile and 107 compressed pieces of various tiger parts.

items.title: How cutting one litre of RON95 daily can save millions: Simple habits to ease Malaysia’s fuel subsidy burden
items.summary: According to available data, Malaysians consume roughly between five and six litres of fuel daily on average. With an estimated 10 million vehicles on the road each day, national fuel consumption is believed to be in the region of 50 to 60 million litres daily. Walking or cycling instead of driving a 2km trip to nearby shops could save about 0.2 to 0.4 litres of fuel.

items.title: Madani Mart vs KR1M: Same same but different?
items.summary: So how will Madani Mart be different? In contrast, Madani Mart operates on a private licensing model, according to Yayasan Madani, the programme’s backer. Yayasan Madani said the Madani Mart will use a “decentralised ecosystem”.

items.title: From sea jacuzzi to pickleball on water: Penang unveils new tourism site Forest Island
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 8 — A new tourism product in Penang will offer visitors experiences in the middle of the sea, ranging from a sea jacuzzi and water sports to scenic views of the island’s coastline. Forest Island is a purpose-built offshore attraction offering sea-based leisure experiences. Visitors enjoy a sea jacuzzi experience during a boat ride to Forest Island.

items.title: Semporna paradise under siege: Locals battle pollution and foreign domination of tourism
items.summary: SEMPORNA, April 8 — On a glossy postcard, Semporna, located on the southeastern coast of Sabah off the Celebes Sea, is sold as the ultimate bucket-list destination. Hordes of tourists, particularly scuba divers and snorkelers, travel thousands of miles to experience the crystal-clear waters and vibrant coral reefs of the islands off Semporna town. Trash For those working on the frontlines of Semporna’s tourism industry, the sea pollution is impossible to ignore.

items.title: Malaysia urged to diversify energy sources amid Middle East crisis and rising oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Rising petroleum import prices and disruptions to global logistics caused by the Middle East conflict, which threaten inflation rates and the country’s long-term fiscal stability, serve as a warning for Malaysia to strengthen energy sovereignty by diversifying local energy sources. Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) vice-chancellor Prof Dr Mohd Zamri Ibrahim said diversifying local energy sources would also reduce the country’s dependence on volatile fossil fuel markets. “Green hydrogen is no longer a ‘technology of the future’ but a present national security necessity.

items.title: In disrupted speech, Azam Baki says strong leadership needed to fight corruption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Effective success in combating corruption does not rely solely on the strength of laws, but requires firm leadership and the cultivation of a comprehensive culture of integrity, says Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki. He said even the most robust system would fail if leadership were weak and compromised with corrupt practices. According to Azam, MACC was implementing a three-pronged approach which encompassed strengthening effective enforcement, comprehensive prevention and efficient management.

items.title: ‘I was not aware’: Dzulkefly denies role in daughter’s board posts amid RM5m suit against Najib
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad told the High Court here today that he was unaware of his eldest daughter Nurul Iman’s appointments to two companies, which allegedly occurred before and after he became minister. Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak arrives at the High Court to continue proceedings in his defamation suit involving Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad over a Facebook post on cronyism, Kuala Lumpur, April 7, 2026. — Bernama pic In his witness statement, Dzulkefly said he had never influenced Nurul Iman’s appointment at AIM Solutions.

items.title: Govt to establish stockpile of medicines, medical devices amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The government will establish a national strategic buffer stock to ensure the supply of medicines and medical devices following the global energy crisis caused by the conflict in West Asia. “The global energy crisis not only affects oil and logistics, but also the supply of medicines and medical devices. In addition, Akmal Nasrullah said the government will implement the Special Access Pathway as a short-term measure to ensure the supply of critical medicines and medical devices in the country remains secure.

items.title: Police: UPSI bowling centre cleaner died of sudden heart attack, no foul play
items.summary: Muallim police chief Supt Khalid Mohamad said the district’s police headquarters received information about the incident via the NG999 system at 1.57am today. He said a police team was dispatched to the scene and found that the Ministry of Health (MOH) and the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) personnel were already there, with the 53-year-old victim found unconscious in a utility room. Preliminary investigation at the scene found no trace of criminal elements or suspicious injuries on the victim’s body.

items.title: Arrest warrants out against Daim Zainuddin’s two sons and their associate for skipping MACC investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The Magistrate’s Court today issued arrest warrants against three individuals, including two sons of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin, for failing to comply with notices from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to face charges today. The prosecution’s application has merit, and therefore, the arrest warrants are issued,” she said. Therefore, MACC filed this application for arrest warrants in court,” said Illi Marisqa.

items.title: B100 pilot shows promise at RM4.50 per litre, Felda plans wider rollout to boost energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — B100, a 100 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, has the potential to emerge as a more competitive and sustainable alternative energy source amid uncertainties arising from the West Asia crisis, said Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek. Ahmad Shabery said he has brought up the matter with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. B100’s factory price is estimated at about RM4.50 per litre, depending on CPO prices, Ahmad Shabery said.

items.title: From housewife to lawyer: Woman without SPM finds purpose after son’s murder charge ordeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A woman’s life changed dramatically after her son was charged in a murder case, prompting her to pursue a law career despite not having completed Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM). Azlina Abdul Aziz, 52, said the 2016 ordeal marked a turning point in her life. Azlina said she was a full-time housewife until her son’s arrest in 2016.

items.title: Kajang man jailed six months after failing to pay fine for abusing kitten at laundry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A 49-year-old man who went viral for throwing a kitten against a wall at a self-service laundry was sentenced to six months in jail today after failing to pay a RM60,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court here. Judge Khainur Aleeza Ismail had earlier sentenced Toh Teck Hooi to the fine and stipulated that failure to pay would result in six months’ imprisonment, according to Harian Metro . The next day, Selangor JPV Animal Welfare officers inspected the site.
